data.headline: Kitchen Remodeling in Westport, CT
data.subheadline: Licensed Connecticut kitchen remodeling contractor serving Westport and Fairfield County — full-service renovations from $30,000 to $150,000+.

data.title: Full-Service Kitchen Remodeling in Westport, CT
data.content:
Kitchen remodeling in Westport typically ranges from $30,000 for a basic cabinet and countertop update to $150,000 or more for a full custom renovation with structural changes. Most projects take six to twelve weeks of active construction, plus cabinet lead time. We hold CT HIC License #0668405 and serve Westport through our Fairfield County Service Area Team.

Westport's residential neighborhoods feature a distinctive mix of colonials, mid-century moderns, and contemporary homes built from the 1950s through the 2000s. Compo Beach, Greens Farms, and Saugatuck areas have homes with kitchens that range from dated 1970s galley layouts to large but aging 1990s designs with dark cabinets and tile countertops.

Westport homeowners expect clear proposals, daily updates during construction, and a project that finishes on the schedule we quoted. That's exactly how we operate.

data.title: What Is Included in a Kitchen Remodel in Westport
data.content:
A full kitchen remodel in Westport covers every trade from demolition through final paint: cabinetry, countertops, tile, flooring, electrical, plumbing, appliance installation, and all required Westport Building Department permits and inspections.

We start with demolition and removal of existing cabinets, countertops, and flooring. Before any new material goes in, we assess what's behind the walls. Westport homes — especially those built in the 1950s through 2000s — regularly turn up undersized electrical panels, deteriorated plumbing, water damage inside wall cavities, or structural framing that needs attention before the renovation can proceed properly.

Cabinetry installation includes soft-close hardware and adjustable shelving as standard. Countertop fabrication and installation, backsplash tile, and underlayment preparation are all part of the project. Electrical scope covers new circuits for appliances, under-cabinet lighting, and updated outlet and switch locations.

Plumbing includes sink and dishwasher connections, garbage disposal installation, and any relocated water lines. We coordinate appliance delivery and installation so you're not managing that separately.

Interior painting, drywall patching, and daily cleanup are included throughout. We install dust barriers to protect the rest of your home during construction. The project closes with a final walkthrough where we go through every detail together.

data.title: What Makes Kitchen Remodeling in Westport Different
data.content: Westport homeowners tend to be design-conscious and want kitchens that reflect current trends while remaining functional for families. Open-concept layouts, large islands, and integrated appliance packages are common requests. Many Westport homes also feature kitchens that open directly to outdoor living spaces — decks, patios, and screened porches — which means the kitchen renovation often needs to consider indoor-outdoor flow and transitions.

data.title: Westport Building Department and Permits
data.content: The Westport Building Department requires permits for all kitchen renovation work involving structural, electrical, or plumbing modifications. Coastal zone regulations apply to many waterfront properties in Compo Beach and Saugatuck Shores. Review times typically run 2-3 weeks. We handle all permit applications and coordinate every required inspection as part of the project scope.

data.title: Recent Kitchen Remodeling Projects
data.subtitle: The projects below give you a sense of what we've completed in Connecticut recently.

items.title: Kitchen Remodeling in New Canaan
items.before_text: A New Canaan family wanted to open up a kitchen closed off by a load-bearing wall. We engineered a beam solution, installed white shaker cabinets with a quartz island, and extended hardwood flooring into the new open layout.

quote.text: BuiltWell made it straightforward. Now we can't imagine how we lived before.
quote.author: The Chens

items.title: Kitchen Remodeling in Milford
items.before_text: A Milford homeowner needed to replace dated 1990s oak cabinets and laminate countertops. We removed the peninsula, installed soft-close cabinetry with quartz countertops and subway tile, and extended LVP flooring into the dining room.

quote.text: They showed up when they said they would, cleaned up every day, and the kitchen turned out better than I imagined.
quote.author: Ivana P.

items.title: Kitchen Remodeling in Westport
items.before_text: A Westport couple wanted a brighter, more functional kitchen. We gutted the space, reconfigured the layout to add a center island, installed custom cabinetry with quartz countertops, and added recessed lighting throughout.

quote.text: We cook together every night now. The island changed everything about how we use the kitchen.
quote.author: The Martins

rows.label: Basic Refresh
rows.notes: Cabinet refacing or stock cabinets, new countertops, updated appliances, paint

rows.label: Mid-Range

rows.label: High-End

data.title: Why Westport Kitchen Remodeling Requires Local Expertise
data.content:
Westport homes present construction conditions that contractors without local experience regularly miss.

Westport kitchens built before 2000 frequently have electrical panels that cannot support modern appliance loads. Many homes in the Greens Farms and Compo areas were built in the 1960s and 1970s with original plumbing and electrical that need updating during a kitchen renovation.

Coastal properties may present additional moisture considerations that affect material selections — particularly for flooring and cabinetry near exterior doors. We account for these conditions in every Westport proposal.

data.title: Our Kitchen Remodeling Process
data.subtitle: Every Westport kitchen remodel follows the same five-step process. This structure keeps projects on schedule, on budget, and clearly communicated throughout.

steps.title: Consultation
steps.short: Visit your Westport home or connect by video
steps.description: We visit your Westport home or connect via Google Meet or Zoom to discuss your goals, assess the space, and answer your questions. No charge. No obligation. We note the existing electrical and plumbing conditions upfront.

steps.title: Planning
steps.short: Clear written proposal and line items
steps.description: You receive a clear written proposal covering exactly what's included, how long it will take, and what it costs. We break out cabinetry, countertops, tile, electrical, plumbing, flooring, and Westport Building Department permits separately so you understand exactly where the budget is going.

steps.title: Selections
steps.short: Materials and lead times locked in early
steps.description: We guide you through material choices with options at different price points. We communicate lead times clearly so selections are made on schedule before construction begins. If a material has a long lead time, we flag that immediately.

steps.title: Build
steps.description: Construction begins on the agreed schedule. You receive daily updates on progress, a clean job site at the end of every workday, and crews who arrive when scheduled. If something unexpected comes up behind a wall, we contact you that day and present your options.

steps.title: Walkthrough
steps.description: We walk through the finished project together. We check every cabinet, every drawer, every tile, every light fixture. If anything needs attention, we address it before calling the project complete. Your written acceptance at the final walkthrough is the last step.

data.title: Project Timeline
data.subtitle: Most kitchen remodels take ten to twenty-two weeks from signed proposal to final walkthrough.

items.title: Planning & Design
items.description: 1-2 Weeks. Consultation, measurements, material selections, and detailed proposal.

items.title: Cabinet Lead Time
items.description: 4-12 Weeks. Custom orders require the longest lead time. Semi-custom options arrive in four to six weeks.

items.title: Construction
items.description: 6-12 Weeks. Demo, rough-ins, cabinetry, countertops, tile, flooring, and fixtures.

items.title: Final Touches
items.description: 1 Week. Hardware, final connections, punch list, and walkthrough.

items.question: Do I need a permit for kitchen remodeling in Westport?
items.answer: Yes. Most kitchen remodeling projects in Westport require permits from the Westport Building Department, particularly when the work involves electrical, plumbing, or structural modifications. We handle all permit applications and coordinate every required inspection as part of the project; you do not need to manage that process yourself.

items.question: How much does kitchen remodeling cost in Westport?
items.answer: Kitchen remodeling in Westport typically ranges from $30,000 for a basic cabinet and countertop refresh to $150,000 or more for a full custom renovation with structural changes. A mid-range project with new cabinets, stone countertops, new flooring, and updated lighting generally falls between $60,000 and $100,000. Your written proposal includes a detailed cost breakdown by trade so you know exactly where the budget is going.

items.question: How long does a kitchen remodel take in Westport?
items.answer: Active construction on a Westport kitchen remodel takes six to twelve weeks depending on the scope of work. Cabinet lead time needs to be added to that figure: custom cabinets require eight to twelve weeks, semi-custom four to six weeks, and stock cabinets are available within one to two weeks. Your written proposal includes a complete project schedule that accounts for all lead times.

items.question: What types of homes do you remodel kitchens in for Westport?
items.answer: Westport's housing stock is primarily colonials, mid-century moderns, and contemporary homes built between the 1950s through 2000s. We're familiar with the construction conditions common in these homes and account for them in every proposal and project scope.

items.question: What is included in a kitchen remodel in Westport?
items.answer: A full-scope kitchen remodel in Westport includes demolition and removal of all existing cabinetry, countertops, and flooring; structural assessment; new cabinetry installation with hardware; countertop fabrication and installation; backsplash tile work; new flooring; all electrical work including new circuits and lighting; all plumbing including sink, dishwasher, and disposal connections; appliance coordination and installation; interior painting; all drywall patching and finish work; Westport Building Department permit applications and inspection coordination; daily cleanup; and a final walkthrough.
